summary:
Cephalometric analyses primarily use reference lines like SN or ANB instead of directly comparing with the mandibular plane; however, certain measurements may involve indirect relations through structures near it such as the Gonion point which helps assess lower jaw positioning but isn't a central axis for full facial skeletal assessment according to standard practices outlined here.
Cranio-facial relationship evaluation via cephalometrics centers more broadly upon established bony markers including sella-nasium alignment over time without relying heavily on absolute positions referenced solely from the mandible base despite potential involvement at peripheral measurement sites related thereto. The main axes considered remain those derived predominantly from skull bases ensuring consistency throughout longitudinal studies focusing specifically not just on one structure but comprehensive face-bone interactions indicative of broader developmental trends observed clinically. To clarify further regarding your query about usage context within these examinations—while ancillary indicators might touch upon aspects linked closely enough geometrically speaking towards mandibles
